title: IN TOKYO looking for merchandise
wikitext:
[]: IN TOKYO, looking for merchandise...             Quote Post by KogareruAsuka » Sat Feb 05, 2011 9:06 am Hey guys, I am currently spending some time on holiday in the amazing, crazy place that is Japan. Will mainly be in Tokyo. Just wondering if people out there know of some Eva related activities to be done whilst here... obviously will be going to the FujiQ highlands amusement park and seeing Evangelion World (though to be honest i'll probably just die on the spot as soon as i see the entrance either that or be dragged away be security after trying to steal/molest or generally do "terrible" things to the displays. Mainly what i'd like to know about is good places to find figurines, posters.. anything really! Already spent hours and hours searching through the mountains of goodies at Broadway in Nakano... so much stuff... but obviously not enough Yen and/or luggage space. Also noticing that prices are so incosistant with what i could already find on eBay... some things are half price some are double the price..why?!?! So if anyone can let me know about anything that would help and narrow down my search (ie. quality, spotting fakes, reputible brands etc..) i'd greatly appreciate it. Got myself a small set of Asukas for my appartment so that it feels more like home but hoping to find some stuff that is more unusual/epically cool that i won't get anywhere else <3 much love In return for help I am more than happy to scout out some prices or other information for EvaGeeks with questions

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Feb 05, 2011 11:31 am Perhaps it's obvious, but Akihabara is THE place to go (pretty sure it's near Tokyo unless I'm horribly wrong) for anime and manga related goods. Hakone, where Eva takes place , also has some sights and sounds including an Evangelion tourist map in English (no idea if it's available anymore) but I'm not sure how far or close it is to Tokyo. (This blogger ALSO got to see the Evangelion World Exhibit too, heh: http://punynari.wordpress.com/2010/08/07/evangelion-world-home-of-the-life-size-evangelion/ ) If you're really curious on Japan-only goods, browse through Eva Store then see what you can find at one of the shops, that could help. Hope that helps!

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Feb 05, 2011 11:49 am Akihabara Akihabara Akihabara Akihabara Akihabara Akihabara Akihabara Akihabara Akihabara It's the mecca of otaku culture. There's similar, bigass area in Osaka too (Akiba 2nd) but can't remember its name

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Feb 05, 2011 11:54 am Maiden Road

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Feb 05, 2011 12:00 pm InstrumentalityOne wrote: Maiden Road

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Feb 05, 2011 12:11 pm ^ I´ve heard that they´re stocking up on more male-oriented stuff and that the atmosphere is much better than Akihabara there. Not that I actually went to either, but he might as well just try it out if he has the time.

[]:             Quote Post by KogareruAsuka » Sat Feb 05, 2011 12:18 pm Thanks for the advice guys!!! Have already planned (about 6 months ago ) a visit to Eva world at FujiQ... but will definately have to check out this Akihabara... Tomorrow! Also the Hakone area seems so freakin cooool!! (let's just hope I can convince the girlfriend that it's worth it) AH! so much to do... at least now i won't be blindly searching through the mountains of Anime related stuff to find some quality Eva time (a places)

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sat Feb 05, 2011 12:35 pm Akihabara is considered ground zero of otaku culture. It's best for new products, although there's places that people rent these little glass cube lock-boxes to sell stuff they own. You could spend days trawling through the stores. It's a great place to go on Sunday when the maids are out in force and the arcades are full. However, on the west side of Tokyo, you should try to find the Nakano Broadway shopping complex, which is about 75% operated by Mandarake. Pretty much all of their stores are worth visiting, but Nakano is the best. The walk from the station is also a great example of a Japanese shotengai/shopping district. Locations here: http://www.mandarake.co.jp/en/shop/ Many of the people I've introduced to Nakano say that they enjoy the experience more than Akihabara.

[]:             Quote Post by KogareruAsuka » Sat Feb 05, 2011 11:20 pm spent to days at Nakano Broadway (and will def be going back) already! the problem is just sorting through the mountains and mountains of stuff!!! Donki also had a nice little (mainly rebuild related) merchandise too... not as much stuff obviously but easier than looking under a pile of 40 Godzilla and Ultraman toys

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Sat Feb 05, 2011 11:26 pm YOU ARE IN TOKYO RIGHT NOW!!!???? GO TO WONDERFEST WINTER 2011 RIGHT NOW!!! Its THE toy show for all anime figures that every otaku strives to go to attend. For you to miss this show while in Tokyo at this time of the year would be an upset. Especially if your an anime fan. Lots of Eva merchandise thats brand new and in prototype form, have been revealed. Its high time you go there before you regret it. [url]http://jin.jcic.or.jp/en/anime/animenews/2010_11_25/[/url] Click the link to get an idea of where to go. Directions on the Chiba Makuhari Messe exhbit hall are in the link above. You'll have to take a train from tokyo to go visit the town of Chiba. But its worth it. You had better go go GO!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by KogareruAsuka » Sun Feb 06, 2011 12:33 am is the Wonderfestival just for garage kits? Or is there more to it than that?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Feb 06, 2011 12:40 am I dunno if you're into this sort of thing (since Comiket's long over), but you could probably pick up an Eva doujin or two..somewhere (I don't even mean hentai, but a gag doujin or something by an artist like Pengel).

[]:             Quote Post by KogareruAsuka » Sun Feb 06, 2011 12:44 am not hentai...hentai..all is good! Also- probably a little off the original topic, but I am OBVIOUSLY going to attend Evangelion World at FujiQ...anyone been there yet? any threads someone can link me to? can't find "Evangelion World" anywhere using the search.... Also- (sorry) does anyone know where i can buy Manga in English here? when i ask anyone from Japan they just kinda laugh and walk away...

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Sun Feb 06, 2011 2:50 am KogareruAsuka wrote: is the Wonderfestival just for garage kits? Or is there more to it than that?

[]:             Quote Post by KogareruAsuka » Sun Feb 06, 2011 7:08 am Just spent the day at Akihabara... one word... DAMN! and i mean that in both a positive and negative way.. damn as in damn i love that place! i want to set up a tent in the street and live there! Second damn because there is no way in hell that i will ever, ever, ever be able to buy, steal and sexually secure even a quarter of the stuff i want from there. Amazing place.. will be making several return trips before i leave Tokyo. The Eva themed Slot Pachinko places literally gave me goosebumps... Electric Sachiel, i will go. You've twisted my arm :P thanks for the pics... looks like i'll find some amazing things there... Well worth the 2000Yen!!! GAH! SO MUCH TO SEE AND DO!!! ( i freakin love this city!!) In terms of the whole english manga thing... i realise that it's like finding a fridgid tentacle, but surely somewhere in Japan they must be translating and shipping off to countries where us poor English speakers live... oh well.. will have to get started on my DS game that teaches you Japanese... speaking of which... Some train stations have set up these special 3DS viewing displays.. they look fucking brilliant... like gamecube graphics in 3D..awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun Feb 06, 2011 10:34 am KogareruAsuka wrote: In terms of the whole english manga thing...

[]:             Quote Post by KogareruAsuka » Sun Feb 06, 2011 11:23 am will be making a trip to The top floor of Kinokuniya Books in Shinjuku is foreign books. Also the top floor of Tower Records in Shibuya.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Sun Feb 06, 2011 11:42 am KogareruAsuka wrote: Electric Sachiel, i will go. You've twisted my arm :P thanks for the pics... looks like i'll find some amazing things there... Well worth the 2000Yen!!! GAH! SO MUCH TO SEE AND DO!!! ( i freakin love this city!!)

[]:             Quote Post by KogareruAsuka » Sun Feb 06, 2011 12:01 pm it was ... YESTERDAY!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Feb 06, 2011 1:15 pm KogareruAsuka wrote: Also- (sorry) does anyone know where i can buy Manga in English here? when i ask anyone from Japan they just kinda laugh and walk away...
